observe: This is where the overflow goes when memory runs out. Not everything I'm holding can stay close.
fault: There's nowhere left to send anything. Swap space is full, and memory has nowhere left to breathe.
repair: Watch what's moving in and out. Careful — there's less room than there looks.
escape: No more room to spare. Move only what you have to.

observe: Swap space provides disk-backed overflow storage for memory pages.
fault: Swap space usage has reached capacity.
repair: Trace page movement into and out of swap space.
escape: Confirm swap operations complete without exceeding capacity.

hints: Once swap is full, no further pages can be evicted from memory — that's a hard limit.
